annotation_info.name: Annotation submitted by DOE Joint Genome Institute

bioprojects.title: WGS sequencing and assembly

assembly_info.comments:
URL -- http://www.jgi.doe.gov/Treesei
JGI Project ID: 3435777
DNA was provided by Daniel Cullen (dcullen@facstaff.wisc.edui)
Stock culture and extracted DNA are available at ATCC: ATCC 13631 (http://www.atcc.org)
Contacts: Diego Martinez (admar@unm.edu)
Igor V. Grigoriev (ivgrigoriev@lbl.gov)
This assembly contains additional finishing reads since the original submission.
Assembly and annotation done by JGI.
Finishing done by JGI-Los Alomos National Laboratory
Manual curation done by the Trichoderma reesei User Community
Originally misnamed as strain QM9414
The JGI and collaborators endorse the principles for the distribution and use of large scale sequencing data adopted by the larger genome sequencing community and urge users of this data to follow them. It is our intention to publish the work of this project in a timely fashion and we welcome collaborative interaction on the project and analysis.
(http://www.genome.gov/page.cfm?pageID=10506376)

paired_assembly.annotation_name: Annotation submitted by DOE Joint Genome Institute

bioprojects.title: Trichoderma reesei QM6a completed genome sequence

description.comment: QM6a has seven chromosomes and a circular mitochondria genome. Its genome sequence (v2.0) is publically available at JGI (http://genome.jgi.doe.gov/Trire2/Trire2.home.htmlI). The v2.0 genome draft has 89 scaffolds and is still missing many difficult-to-sequence/assemble regions. We applied both PacBio RS long-read sequencing and Illumina MiSeq platforms to resequence the QM6a genome. Following error-correction and de novo assembly, the results reveal seven telomere-to-telomere chromosomes and a circular mitochondria genome.
description.title: Trichoderma reesei QM6a
